chapter_title.en: Chapter 69 – Receiving the goods outside the city

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/161)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/161)
text: قُلْتُ لَهُ مَا حَدُّ التَّلَقِّي قَالَ رَوْحَةٌ .

en.hubeali: Ibn Mahboub, from Abdullah Bin Yahya Al Kahily, from Minhal Al Qassab who said,
‘I said to himasws, ‘What is the limit of the receipt of trade outside the city?’ Heasws said: ‘A Rawha (four Fasakhs – about six miles)’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ibn Mahbub has narrated from ‘Abd Allah ibn Yahya al-Kahily from Minhal al-Qassab who has said the following: “I once asked him (the Imam), ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘What is the limit of going out to meet a caravan of suppliers?’ He (the Imam) replied, ‘It is a distance of one Rawhah (four Farasikh which is equal to six miles)”’
